questions.question_text: How many members serve on the Georgia Real Estate Commission?
questions.explanation: GREC has six members: five licensees and one consumer member. The inclusion of a consumer member ensures public representation on the Commission alongside industry professionals.

questions.question_text: A newly appointed GREC member asks how the chairperson is selected. What is the correct process?
questions.correct_answer: The Commission elects its own chairperson
distractors: The Secretary of State selects the chairperson
questions.explanation: GREC members elect their own chairperson from among themselves. This internal selection process gives the Commission autonomy in choosing its leadership, rather than relying on external appointment.

questions.question_text: The Governor nominates a retired teacher with no real estate license to GREC. Which seat could this person fill?
distractors: Any of the six seats on the Commission
None, all members must be licensees
questions.explanation: One of the six GREC seats is reserved for a consumer member who is not a licensee. The remaining five seats must be filled by active real estate licensees, so a non-licensee can only fill the consumer seat.

questions.question_text: GREC members serve staggered terms of what length?
questions.explanation: GREC members serve five-year staggered terms. Staggering ensures continuity so that all seats do not expire simultaneously, preserving institutional knowledge on the Commission.

questions.question_text: After the Governor appoints a new GREC member, which body must confirm the appointment before the member can serve?
questions.explanation: GREC appointments by the Governor must be confirmed by the Georgia Senate. This dual-step process provides legislative oversight of who regulates the real estate industry.

questions.question_text: GREC discovers an unlicensed person conducting real estate closings for a fee. Which enforcement power should GREC exercise first?
questions.correct_answer: Issue a cease and desist order
distractors: Impose a fine on the person's broker
Refer the matter to the Recovery Fund
questions.explanation: GREC can issue cease and desist orders to stop unlicensed activity immediately. License revocation would not apply here because the person has no license, making a cease and desist the appropriate initial action.

questions.question_text: Which Georgia statute grants GREC its primary regulatory authority over real estate licensees?
questions.explanation: O.C.G.A. Section 43-40 is the Georgia Real Estate License Law and the primary statute granting GREC authority. Other statutes govern different aspects of Georgia law unrelated to real estate licensing.

questions.question_text: A community association manager (CAM) in Georgia questions whether GREC has authority over their license. Does GREC have jurisdiction?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, CAMs fall under GREC jurisdiction
distractors: No, CAMs are regulated by a separate board
Only if the CAM also holds a broker license
questions.explanation: GREC has jurisdiction over community association managers in addition to salespersons, brokers, associate brokers, and firms. CAM licensing is specifically included under GREC's regulatory authority in Georgia.

questions.question_text: GREC suspects a brokerage is commingling earnest money deposits. Which power allows GREC to verify this without a formal complaint?
questions.explanation: GREC has the power to conduct trust account audits to ensure brokerages properly handle client funds. This authority can be exercised proactively and does not require a formal complaint to initiate.

questions.question_text: Which types of professionals does GREC have licensing authority over?
questions.correct_answer: Brokers, salespersons, associate brokers, CAMs, and firms
distractors: All real estate and mortgage loan professionals
questions.explanation: GREC's jurisdiction covers salespersons, brokers, associate brokers, community association managers, and real estate firms. Appraisers and mortgage professionals are regulated by separate Georgia boards.

questions.question_text: GREC operates as a division under which Georgia state office?
questions.correct_answer: Secretary of State's Licensing Boards Division
distractors: Office of the Georgia Attorney General
questions.explanation: GREC falls under the Secretary of State's Professional Licensing Boards Division. This administrative placement groups real estate regulation with other professional licensing functions in Georgia.

questions.question_text: A licensee calls GREC about a routine application status. Who handles this day-to-day inquiry?
questions.correct_answer: The Real Estate Commissioner on staff
questions.explanation: The Real Estate Commissioner is a staff position that handles day-to-day operations of GREC. This person is not the same as the appointed Commission members who set policy and make disciplinary decisions.

questions.question_text: GREC adopts a new regulation that conflicts with an existing state statute. Which authority takes precedence?
questions.correct_answer: The state statute under O.C.G.A. Section 43-40
distractors: The GREC regulation as the more recent action
Whichever authority the Commission chair selects
The Secretary of State resolves any conflicts
questions.explanation: GREC's rulemaking authority is delegated by the legislature. Commission rules cannot exceed or conflict with the enabling statute at O.C.G.A. Section 43-40. Statutes always take precedence over administrative regulations.

questions.question_text: The GREC Commission votes to adopt a new trust account rule. Who is responsible for implementing that rule in daily operations?
questions.correct_answer: The Real Estate Commissioner and staff
questions.explanation: While the Commission sets policy through rulemaking, the Real Estate Commissioner (a staff position) and their team handle implementation and day-to-day enforcement. This separation keeps policy and operations distinct.

questions.question_text: What is the key distinction between the Real Estate Commissioner and GREC Commission members?
questions.correct_answer: The Commissioner is staff; members are appointed
distractors: The Commissioner serves longer terms than members
The Commissioner must be an active broker
Members handle operations while Commissioner sets policy
questions.explanation: The Real Estate Commissioner is a staff employee managing day-to-day operations, while Commission members are Governor-appointed officials who set policy and make disciplinary decisions. Confusing these two roles is a common exam mistake.
